title: Eria pachyphylla
taxonomy: Eria pachyphylla is a species of orchid, belonging to the genus Eria and the family Orchidaceae. It is a type of epiphytic orchid, which means it grows on other plants for support. The Orchidaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, consisting of approximately 30,000 known species. Eria pachyphylla is further classified into various ranks, including kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and genetic characteristics.

intro: Eria pachyphylla is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal properties and unique appearance. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Eria pachyphylla contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Eria pachyphylla is often prepared as a decoction or infused in tea, and also used in the form of capsules or tablets, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues.
